verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his father's wife, for he dishonors his father's bed.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who lies with any kind of animal.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who has sexual relations with any animal.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who lies with his sister, his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his sister, the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who lies with his mother-in-law.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his mother-in-law.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who secretly kills his neighbor.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who kills their neighbor secretly.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who takes a bribe to kill an innocent person.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who accepts a bribe to kill an innocent person.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

verses.text:
‘Cursed is he who doesn’t uphold the words of this law by doing them.’
All the people shall say, ‘Amen.’”
verses.explainer: 'Cursed is anyone who does not uphold the words of this law by carrying them out.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

crossReferences.reference: Galatians 3:10 -- Paul quotes this verse: 'Cursed is everyone who does not continue to do everything written in the Book of the Law.' He then argues that Christ redeemed us from this curse.
crossReferences.connection: Galatians 3:10 -- Paul quotes this verse: 'Cursed is everyone who does not continue to do everything written in the Book of the Law.' He then argues that Christ redeemed us from this curse.

explainer.summary: The longest and most intense chapter in Deuteronomy presents the consequences of covenant obedience and disobedience. The blessings (vv.1-14) are comprehensive: fertility, prosperity, victory, and international prestige. The curses (vv.15-68) are far longer and more detailed: disease, drought, defeat, madness, exile, and ultimately the horror of siege warfare. The chapter culminates with a return to Egypt in ships -- the reversal of the Exodus, the undoing of everything God accomplished.
themes: Comprehensive blessings for obedience
Devastating curses for disobedience
Covenant consequences are real and specific
The horror of siege and exile
Return to Egypt as the ultimate reversal
explainer.contextInBook: Chapter 28 is the climax of the covenant ceremony. Everything in Deuteronomy builds toward this choice: blessing or curse, life or death. The disproportionate length of the curses (54 verses) compared to the blessings (14 verses) reflects Moses' realistic assessment that Israel will more likely fail than succeed.

wordStudies.significance: The word 'barukh' (blessed) appears in the blessings section, describing a state of divine favor that pervades every area of life: city, field, womb, livestock, basket, and kneading trough. Blessing is comprehensive, not compartmentalized.

verses.text: It shall happen, if you shall listen diligently to Yahweh your God’s voice, to observe to do all his commandments which I command you today, that Yahweh your God will set you high above all the nations of the earth.
verses.explainer: If you fully obey the Lord your God and carefully follow all his commands I give you today, the Lord will set you high above all the nations on earth.

verses.text: All these blessings will come upon you, and overtake you, if you listen to Yahweh your God’s voice.
verses.explainer: All these blessings will come on you and accompany you if you obey the Lord your God.

verses.text: You shall be blessed in the city, and you shall be blessed in the field.
verses.explainer: You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the country.

verses.text: You shall be blessed in the fruit of your body, the fruit of your ground, the fruit of your animals, the increase of your livestock, and the young of your flock.
verses.explainer: The fruit of your womb will be blessed, and the crops of your land and the young of your livestock -- the calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks.

verses.text: Your basket and your kneading trough shall be blessed.
verses.explainer: Your basket and your kneading trough will be blessed.

verses.text: You shall be blessed when you come in, and you shall be blessed when you go out.
verses.explainer: You will be blessed when you come in and blessed when you go out.

verses.text: Yahweh will cause your enemies who rise up against you to be struck before you. They will come out against you one way, and will flee before you seven ways.
verses.explainer: The Lord will grant that the enemies who rise up against you will be defeated before you.

verses.text: Yahweh will command the blessing on you in your barns, and in all that you put your hand to. He will bless you in the land which Yahweh your God gives you.
verses.explainer: The Lord will send a blessing on your barns and on everything you put your hand to. The Lord will bless you in the land he is giving you.

verses.text: Yahweh will establish you for a holy people to himself, as he has sworn to you, if you shall keep the commandments of Yahweh your God, and walk in his ways.
verses.explainer: The Lord will establish you as his holy people, as he promised you on oath, if you keep the commands of the Lord your God and walk in obedience to him.

verses.text: All the peoples of the earth shall see that you are called by Yahweh’s name, and they will be afraid of you.
verses.explainer: Then all the peoples on earth will see that you are called by the name of the Lord, and they will fear you.

verses.text: Yahweh will grant you abundant prosperity, in the fruit of your body, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your ground, in the land which Yahweh swore to your fathers to give you.
verses.explainer: The Lord will grant you abundant prosperity -- in the fruit of your womb, the young of your livestock and the crops of your ground -- in the land he swore to your ancestors to give you.

verses.text: Yahweh will open to you his good treasure in the sky, to give the rain of your land in its season, and to bless all the work of your hand. You will lend to many nations, and you will not borrow.
verses.explainer: The Lord will open the heavens, the storehouse of his bounty, to send rain on your land in season and to bless all the work of your hands. You will lend to many nations but will borrow from none.

verses.text: Yahweh will make you the head, and not the tail. You will be above only, and you will not be beneath; if you listen to the commandments of Yahweh your God, which I command you today, to observe and to do,
verses.explainer: The Lord will make you the head, not the tail. If you pay attention to the commands of the Lord your God that I give you this day and carefully follow them, you will always be at the top, never at the bottom.

verses.text: and shall not turn away from any of the words which I command you today, to the right hand, or to the left, to go after other gods to serve them.
verses.explainer: Do not turn aside from any of the commands I give you today, to the right or to the left, following other gods and serving them.

verses.text: But it shall come to pass, if you will not listen to Yahweh your God’s voice, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I command you today, that all these curses will come on you, and overtake you.
verses.explainer: However, if you do not obey the Lord your God and do not carefully follow all his commands and decrees I am giving you today, all these curses will come on you and overtake you.

verses.text: You will be cursed in the city, and you will be cursed in the field.
verses.explainer: You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the country.

verses.text: Your basket and your kneading trough will be cursed.
verses.explainer: Your basket and your kneading trough will be cursed.

verses.text: The fruit of your body, the fruit of your ground, the increase of your livestock, and the young of your flock will be cursed.
verses.explainer: The fruit of your womb will be cursed, and the crops of your land, and the calves of your herds and the lambs of your flocks.

verses.text: You will be cursed when you come in, and you will be cursed when you go out.
verses.explainer: You will be cursed when you come in and cursed when you go out.

verses.text: Yahweh will send on you cursing, confusion, and rebuke, in all that you put your hand to do, until you are destroyed, and until you perish quickly; because of the evil of your doings, by which you have forsaken me.
verses.explainer: The Lord will send on you curses, confusion, and rebuke in everything you put your hand to, until you are destroyed and come to sudden ruin.

verses.text: Yahweh will make the pestilence cling to you, until he has consumed you from off the land, where you go in to possess it.
verses.explainer: The Lord will plague you with diseases until he has destroyed you from the land you are entering to possess.

verses.text: Yahweh will strike you with consumption, with fever, with inflammation, with fiery heat, with the sword, with blight, and with mildew. They will pursue you until you perish.
verses.explainer: The Lord will strike you with wasting disease, with fever and inflammation, with scorching heat and drought, with blight and mildew, which will plague you until you perish.

verses.text: Your sky that is over your head will be brass, and the earth that is under you will be iron.
verses.explainer: The sky over your head will be bronze, the ground beneath you iron.

thenVsNow.then: Bronze sky (no rain) and iron ground (no harvest) describe total agricultural failure. For a rain-dependent agricultural society, this is the end of everything.
thenVsNow.now: The imagery of heaven closed and earth barren captures the experience of spiritual desolation -- when God seems absent and life produces nothing of value.

verses.text: Yahweh will make the rain of your land powder and dust. It will come down on you from the sky, until you are destroyed.
verses.explainer: The Lord will turn the rain of your country into dust and powder; it will come down from the skies until you are destroyed.

verses.text: Yahweh will cause you to be struck before your enemies. You will go out one way against them, and will flee seven ways before them. You will be tossed back and forth among all the kingdoms of the earth.
verses.explainer: The Lord will cause you to be defeated before your enemies.

verses.text: Your dead body will be food to all birds of the sky, and to the animals of the earth; and there will be no one to frighten them away.
verses.explainer: Your carcasses will be food for all the birds and the wild animals, and there will be no one to frighten them away.

verses.text: Yahweh will strike you with the boils of Egypt, with the tumors, with the scurvy, and with the itch, of which you can not be healed.
verses.explainer: The Lord will afflict you with the boils of Egypt and with tumors, festering sores, and the itch, from which you cannot be cured.

verses.text: Yahweh will strike you with madness, with blindness, and with astonishment of heart.
verses.explainer: The Lord will afflict you with madness, blindness, and confusion of mind.

verses.text: You will grope at noonday, as the blind gropes in darkness, and you shall not prosper in your ways. You will only be oppressed and robbed always, and there will be no one to save you.
verses.explainer: At midday you will grope about like a blind person in the dark. You will be unsuccessful in everything you do; day after day you will be oppressed and robbed, with no one to rescue you.

verses.text: You will betroth a wife, and another man shall lie with her. You will build a house, and you won’t dwell in it. You will plant a vineyard, and not use its fruit.
verses.explainer: You will be pledged to be married to a woman, but another will take her and rape her. You will build a house, but you will not live in it. You will plant a vineyard, but you will not even begin to enjoy its fruit.

verses.text: Your ox will be slain before your eyes, and you will not eat any of it. Your donkey will be violently taken away from before your face, and will not be restored to you. Your sheep will be given to your enemies, and you will have no one to save you.
verses.explainer: Your ox will be slaughtered before your eyes, but you will eat none of it. Your donkey will be forcibly taken from you and will not be returned.

verses.text: Your sons and your daughters will be given to another people. Your eyes will look, and fail with longing for them all day long. There will be no power in your hand.
verses.explainer: Your sons and daughters will be given to another nation, and you will wear out your eyes watching for them day after day, powerless to lift a hand.

verses.text: A nation which you don’t know will eat the fruit of your ground and all of your work. You will only be oppressed and crushed always;
verses.explainer: A people that you do not know will eat what your land and labor produce, and you will have nothing but cruel oppression all your days.

verses.text: so that the sights that you see with your eyes will drive you mad.
verses.explainer: The sights you see will drive you mad.

verses.text: Yahweh will strike you in the knees and in the legs with a sore boil, of which you can not be healed, from the sole of your foot to the crown of your head.
verses.explainer: The Lord will afflict your knees and legs with painful boils that cannot be cured, spreading from the soles of your feet to the top of your head.

verses.text: Yahweh will bring you, and your king whom you will set over yourselves, to a nation that you have not known, you nor your fathers. There you will serve other gods of wood and stone.
verses.explainer: The Lord will drive you and the king you set over you to a nation unknown to you or your ancestors. There you will worship other gods, gods of wood and stone.

verses.text: You will become an astonishment, a proverb, and a byword among all the peoples where Yahweh will lead you away.
verses.explainer: You will become a thing of horror, a byword and an object of ridicule among all the peoples where the Lord will drive you.

verses.text: You will carry much seed out into the field, and will gather little in; for the locust will consume it.
verses.explainer: You will sow much seed in the field but you will harvest little, because locusts will devour it.

verses.text: You will plant vineyards and dress them, but you will neither drink of the wine, nor harvest, because worms will eat them.
verses.explainer: You will plant vineyards and cultivate them but you will not drink the wine or gather the grapes, because worms will eat them.

verses.text: You will have olive trees throughout all your borders, but you won’t anoint yourself with the oil; for your olives will drop off.
verses.explainer: You will have olive trees throughout your country but you will not use the oil, because the olives will drop off.

verses.text: You will father sons and daughters, but they will not be yours; for they will go into captivity.
verses.explainer: You will have sons and daughters but you will not keep them, because they will go into captivity.

verses.text: Locusts will consume all of your trees and the fruit of your ground.
verses.explainer: Swarms of locusts will take over all your trees and the crops of your land.

verses.text: The foreigner who is among you will mount up above you higher and higher, and you will come down lower and lower.
verses.explainer: The foreigners who reside among you will rise above you higher and higher, but you will sink lower and lower.

verses.text: He will lend to you, and you won’t lend to him. He will be the head, and you will be the tail.
verses.explainer: They will lend to you, but you will not lend to them. They will be the head, but you will be the tail.

verses.text: All these curses will come on you, and will pursue you, and overtake you, until you are destroyed; because you didn’t listen to Yahweh your God’s voice, to keep his commandments and his statutes which he commanded you.
verses.explainer: All these curses will come on you. They will pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed, because you did not obey the Lord your God.

verses.text: They will be for a sign and for a wonder to you and to your offspring forever.
verses.explainer: They will be a sign and a wonder to you and your descendants forever.

verses.text: Because you didn’t serve Yahweh your God with joyfulness, and with gladness of heart, by reason of the abundance of all things;
verses.explainer: Because you did not serve the Lord your God joyfully and gladly in the time of prosperity.

verses.text: therefore you will serve your enemies whom Yahweh sends against you, in hunger, in thirst, in nakedness, and in lack of all things. He will put an iron yoke on your neck, until he has destroyed you.
verses.explainer: Therefore in hunger and thirst, in nakedness and dire poverty, you will serve the enemies the Lord sends against you.

verses.text: Yahweh will bring a nation against you from far, from the end of the earth, as the eagle flies; a nation whose language you will not understand;
verses.explainer: The Lord will bring a nation against you from far away, from the ends of the earth, like an eagle swooping down, a nation whose language you will not understand.

verses.text: a nation of fierce facial expressions, that doesn’t respect the elderly, nor show favor to the young,
verses.explainer: A fierce-looking nation without respect for the old or pity for the young.

verses.text: and they will eat the fruit of your livestock, and the fruit of your ground, until you are destroyed. They also won’t leave you grain, new wine, or oil, the increase of your livestock, or the young of your flock, until they have caused you to perish.
verses.explainer: They will devour the young of your livestock and the crops of your land until you are destroyed.

verses.text: They will besiege you in all your gates, until your high and fortified walls come down, in which you trusted, throughout all your land. They will besiege you in all your gates throughout all your land, which Yahweh your God has given you.
verses.explainer: They will lay siege to all the cities throughout your land until the high fortified walls in which you trust fall down.

verses.text: You will eat the fruit of your own body, the flesh of your sons and of your daughters, whom Yahweh your God has given you, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemies will distress you.
verses.explainer: Because of the suffering your enemy will inflict on you during the siege, you will eat the fruit of the womb, the flesh of the sons and daughters the Lord your God has given you.

crossReferences.reference: 2 Kings 6:28-29 -- During the siege of Samaria, this curse was literally fulfilled: women resorted to cannibalism. Lamentations 2:20 records the same during the Babylonian siege of Jerusalem.
crossReferences.connection: 2 Kings 6:28-29 -- During the siege of Samaria, this curse was literally fulfilled: women resorted to cannibalism. Lamentations 2:20 records the same during the Babylonian siege of Jerusalem.

verses.text: The man who is tender among you, and very delicate, his eye will be evil toward his brother, toward the wife whom he loves, and toward the remnant of his children whom he has remaining;
verses.explainer: Even the most gentle and sensitive man among you will have no compassion on his own brother or the wife he loves or his surviving children.

verses.text: so that he will not give to any of them of the flesh of his children whom he will eat, because he has nothing left to him, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy will distress you in all your gates.
verses.explainer: And he will not give to one of them any of the flesh of his children that he is eating. It will be all he has left because of the suffering your enemy will inflict on you during the siege of all your cities.

verses.text: The tender and delicate woman among you, who would not venture to set the sole of her foot on the ground for delicateness and tenderness, her eye will be evil toward the husband that she loves, toward her son, toward her daughter,
verses.explainer: The most gentle and sensitive woman among you -- so sensitive and gentle that she would not venture to touch the ground with the sole of her foot --

verses.text: toward her young one who comes out from between her feet, and toward her children whom she bears; for she will eat them secretly for lack of all things, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy will distress you in your gates.
verses.explainer: Will begrudge the husband she loves and her own son or daughter the afterbirth from her womb and the children she bears. For in her dire need she intends to eat them secretly because of the suffering your enemy will inflict on you during the siege.

thenVsNow.then: The horror of siege cannibalism -- a refined woman eating her own children -- represents the absolute nadir of human degradation. Moses' graphic description is intentionally devastating.
thenVsNow.now: These verses are nearly unbearable to read, which is precisely their purpose. They are meant to shock Israel into understanding what covenant betrayal will cost. The curses are not punitive fantasies but prophetic warnings that were historically fulfilled.

verses.text: If you will not observe to do all the words of this law that are written in this book, that you may fear this glorious and fearful name, YAHWEH YOUR GOD;
verses.explainer: If you do not carefully follow all the words of this law, which are written in this book, and do not revere this glorious and awesome name -- the Lord your God --

verses.text: then Yahweh will make your plagues fearful, and the plagues of your offspring, even great plagues, and of long duration, and severe sicknesses, and of long duration.
verses.explainer: The Lord will send fearful plagues on you and your descendants, harsh and prolonged disasters, and severe and lingering illnesses.

verses.text: He will bring on you again all the diseases of Egypt, which you were afraid of; and they will cling to you.
verses.explainer: He will bring on you all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded, and they will cling to you.

verses.text: Also every sickness and every plague, which is not written in the book of this law, Yahweh will bring them on you, until you are destroyed.
verses.explainer: The Lord will also bring on you every kind of sickness and disaster not recorded in this Book of the Law, until you are destroyed.

verses.text: You will be left few in number, even though you were as the stars of the sky for multitude; because you didn’t listen to Yahweh your God’s voice.
verses.explainer: You who were as numerous as the stars in the sky will be left but few in number, because you did not obey the Lord your God.

verses.text: It will happen that as Yahweh rejoiced over you to do you good, and to multiply you, so Yahweh will rejoice over you to cause you to perish, and to destroy you. You will be plucked from the land that you are going in to possess.
verses.explainer: Just as it pleased the Lord to make you prosper and increase in number, so it will please him to ruin and destroy you. You will be uprooted from the land you are entering to possess.

verses.text: Yahweh will scatter you among all peoples, from one end of the earth to the other end of the earth. There you will serve other gods, which you have not known, you nor your fathers, even wood and stone.
verses.explainer: Then the Lord will scatter you among all nations, from one end of the earth to the other.

verses.text: Among these nations you will find no ease, and there will be no rest for the sole of your foot; but Yahweh will give you there a trembling heart, failing of eyes, and pining of soul.
verses.explainer: Among those nations you will find no repose, no resting place for the sole of your foot. There the Lord will give you an anxious mind, eyes weary with longing, and a despairing heart.

verses.text: Your life will hang in doubt before you. You will be afraid night and day, and will have no assurance of your life.
verses.explainer: You will live in constant suspense, filled with dread both night and day, never sure of your life.

verses.text: In the morning you will say, “I wish it were evening!” and at evening you will say, “I wish it were morning!” for the fear of your heart which you will fear, and for the sights which your eyes will see.
verses.explainer: In the morning you will say, 'If only it were evening!' and in the evening, 'If only it were morning!' -- because of the terror that will fill your hearts and the sights that your eyes will see.

verses.text: Yahweh will bring you into Egypt again with ships, by the way of which I told to you that you would never see it again. There you will sell yourselves to your enemies for male and female slaves, and nobody will buy you.
verses.explainer: The Lord will send you back in ships to Egypt on a journey I said you should never make again. There you will offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but no one will buy you.

explainer.summary: Moses begins his third and final speech by renewing the covenant with the generation on the plains of Moab. He recounts God's faithfulness through forty years of wilderness, reminds them that God gave them eyes to see but they have not yet truly perceived. He warns against anyone who thinks they can privately violate the covenant while publicly appearing faithful. The chapter concludes with one of the most quoted statements in Deuteronomy: 'The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our children forever.'
themes: Covenant renewal for the current generation
Seeing without perceiving -- spiritual blindness
No private exemptions from the covenant
The danger of self-deception
Secret things belong to God; revealed things belong to us
explainer.contextInBook: Moses' third speech (chapters 29-30) is the covenant renewal ceremony itself. Having reviewed history (speech 1) and restated the law (speech 2), Moses now calls the nation to formally recommit to the covenant with full knowledge of the blessings and curses.

wordStudies.significance: The 'nistarot' (hidden things) belong to God alone. This verse (29:29) establishes a boundary between what God reveals (which Israel must obey) and what God keeps hidden (which Israel must accept). It is a statement about the limits of human knowledge and the sufficiency of divine revelation.

verses.text: These are the words of the covenant which Yahweh commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, in addition to the covenant which he made with them in Horeb.
verses.explainer: These are the terms of the covenant the Lord commanded Moses to make with the Israelites in Moab, in addition to the covenant he had made with them at Horeb.

verses.text:
Moses called to all Israel, and said to them:
Your eyes have seen all that Yahweh did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh, and to all his servants, and to all his land;
verses.explainer: Moses summons all the Israelites and says: your eyes have seen all that the Lord did in Egypt to Pharaoh, to all his officials and to all his land.

verses.text: the great trials which your eyes saw, the signs, and those great wonders.
verses.explainer: With your own eyes you saw those great trials, those signs and great wonders.

verses.text: But Yahweh has not given you a heart to know, eyes to see, and ears to hear, to this day.
verses.explainer: But to this day the Lord has not given you a mind that understands or eyes that see or ears that hear.

crossReferences.reference: Isaiah 6:9-10 -- Isaiah's commission echoes this: 'Be ever hearing, but never understanding; be ever seeing, but never perceiving.' Jesus quotes this in Matthew 13:14-15.
crossReferences.connection: Isaiah 6:9-10 -- Isaiah's commission echoes this: 'Be ever hearing, but never understanding; be ever seeing, but never perceiving.' Jesus quotes this in Matthew 13:14-15.

crossReferences.reference: Romans 11:8 -- Paul cites this verse when discussing Israel's spiritual blindness.
crossReferences.connection: Romans 11:8 -- Paul cites this verse when discussing Israel's spiritual blindness.

verses.text: I have led you forty years in the wilderness. Your clothes have not grown old on you, and your shoes have not grown old on your feet.
verses.explainer: Yet the Lord says: 'During the forty years that I led you through the wilderness, your clothes did not wear out, nor did the sandals on your feet.'

verses.text: You have not eaten bread, neither have you drunk wine or strong drink; that you may know that I am Yahweh your God.
verses.explainer: You ate no bread and drank no wine or other fermented drink. I did this so that you might know that I am the Lord your God.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: The reference to not eating bread or drinking wine in the wilderness emphasizes their total dependence on manna and water from the rock. God supplied everything so they would know him as provider.

verses.text: When you came to this place, Sihon the king of Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, came out against us to battle, and we struck them.
verses.explainer: When you reached this place, Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan came out to fight against us, but we defeated them.

verses.text: We took their land, and gave it for an inheritance to the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the half-tribe of the Manassites.
verses.explainer: We took their land and gave it as an inheritance to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.

verses.text: Therefore keep the words of this covenant and do them, that you may prosper in all that you do.
verses.explainer: Carefully follow the terms of this covenant, so that you may prosper in everything you do.

verses.text: All of you stand today in the presence of Yahweh your God; your heads, your tribes, your elders, and your officers, even all the men of Israel,
verses.explainer: All of you are standing today in the presence of the Lord your God -- your leaders and chief men, your elders and officials, and all the other men of Israel.

verses.text: your little ones, your wives, and the foreigners who are in the middle of your camps, from the one who cuts your wood to the one who draws your water;
verses.explainer: Together with your children and your wives, and the foreigners living in your camps who chop your wood and carry your water.

thenVsNow.then: The covenant includes everyone: leaders, men, women, children, and even foreign laborers. No one is excluded from covenant membership based on social status.
thenVsNow.now: The inclusiveness of the covenant assembly challenges any community that restricts participation based on status. If wood-choppers and water-carriers are included, no one can be excluded.

verses.text: that you may enter into the covenant of Yahweh your God, and into his oath, which Yahweh your God makes with you today;
verses.explainer: You are standing here in order to enter into a covenant with the Lord your God, a covenant the Lord is making with you this day and sealing with an oath.

verses.text: that he may establish you today as his people, and that he may be your God, as he spoke to you, and as he swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
verses.explainer: He is confirming you this day as his people, that he may be your God as he promised you and as he swore to your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

verses.text: Neither do I make this covenant and this oath with you only,
verses.explainer: I am making this covenant, with its oath, not only with you who are standing here with us today in the presence of the Lord our God --

verses.text: but with those who stand here with us today before Yahweh our God, and also with those who are not here with us today
verses.explainer: But also with those who are not here today.

verses.text: (for you know how we lived in the land of Egypt, and how we came through the middle of the nations through which you passed;
verses.explainer: You yourselves know how we lived in Egypt and how we passed through the countries on the way here.

verses.text: and you have seen their abominations, and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold, which were among them);
verses.explainer: You saw among them their detestable images and idols of wood and stone, of silver and gold.

verses.text: lest there should be among you man, woman, family, or tribe whose heart turns away today from Yahweh our God, to go to serve the gods of those nations; lest there should be among you a root that produces bitter poison;
verses.explainer: Make sure there is no man or woman, clan or tribe among you today whose heart turns away from the Lord our God to go and worship the gods of those nations; make sure there is no root among you that produces such bitter poison.

crossReferences.reference: Hebrews 12:15 -- The author of Hebrews alludes to this verse: 'See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God and that no bitter root grows up to cause trouble and defile many.'
crossReferences.connection: Hebrews 12:15 -- The author of Hebrews alludes to this verse: 'See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God and that no bitter root grows up to cause trouble and defile many.'

verses.text: and it happen, when he hears the words of this curse, that he bless himself in his heart, saying, “I shall have peace, though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart, to destroy the moist with the dry.”
verses.explainer: When such a person hears the words of this oath and they invoke a blessing on themselves, thinking, 'I will be safe, even though I persist in going my own way,' they will bring disaster on the watered land as well as the dry.

verses.text: Yahweh will not pardon him, but then Yahweh’s anger and his jealousy will smoke against that man, and all the curse that is written in this book will fall on him, and Yahweh will blot out his name from under the sky.
verses.explainer: The Lord will never be willing to forgive them; his wrath and zeal will burn against them. All the curses written in this book will fall on them, and the Lord will blot out their names from under heaven.

verses.text: Yahweh will set him apart for evil out of all the tribes of Israel, according to all the curses of the covenant that is written in this book of the law.
verses.explainer: The Lord will single them out from all the tribes of Israel for disaster, according to all the curses of the covenant written in this Book of the Law.

verses.text: The generation to come, your children who will rise up after you, and the foreigner who will come from a far land, will say, when they see the plagues of that land, and the sicknesses with which Yahweh has made it sick;
verses.explainer: Your children who follow you in later generations and foreigners who come from distant lands will see the calamities that have fallen on the land and the diseases with which the Lord has afflicted it.

verses.text: and that all of its land is sulfur, salt, and burning, that it is not sown, doesn’t produce, nor does any grass grow in it, like the overthrow of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, which Yahweh overthrew in his anger, and in his wrath;
verses.explainer: The whole land will be a burning waste of salt and sulfur -- nothing planted, nothing sprouting, no vegetation growing on it. It will be like the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboyim, which the Lord overthrew in fierce anger.

crossReferences.reference: Genesis 19:24-25 -- The destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah serves as the paradigmatic example of total divine judgment throughout Scripture.
crossReferences.connection: Genesis 19:24-25 -- The destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah serves as the paradigmatic example of total divine judgment throughout Scripture.

verses.text: even all the nations will say, “Why has Yahweh done thus to this land? What does the heat of this great anger mean?”
verses.explainer: All the nations will ask: 'Why has the Lord done this to this land? Why this fierce, burning anger?'

verses.text: Then men will say, “Because they abandoned the covenant of Yahweh, the God of their fathers, which he made with them when he brought them out of the land of Egypt,
verses.explainer: And the answer will be: 'It is because this people abandoned the covenant of the Lord, the God of their ancestors, the covenant he made with them when he brought them out of Egypt.'

verses.text: and went and served other gods, and worshiped them, gods that they didn’t know, and that he had not given to them.
verses.explainer: They went off and worshiped other gods and bowed down to them, gods they did not know, gods he had not given them.

verses.text: Therefore Yahweh’s anger burned against this land, to bring on it all the curses that are written in this book.
verses.explainer: Therefore the Lord's anger burned against this land, so that he brought on it all the curses written in this book.

verses.text: Yahweh rooted them out of their land in anger, in wrath, and in great indignation, and thrust them into another land, as it is today.”
verses.explainer: In furious anger and in great wrath the Lord uprooted them from their land and thrust them into another land, as it is now.

verses.text: The secret things belong to Yahweh our God; but the things that are revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may do all the words of this law.
verses.explainer: The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our children forever, that we may follow all the words of this law.

thenVsNow.then: Israel was not called to comprehend all of God's ways but to obey what God had revealed. The distinction between 'secret' and 'revealed' sets healthy boundaries on theological inquiry.
thenVsNow.now: This verse provides both comfort and challenge: comfort because we are not expected to understand everything, challenge because we are fully accountable for what has been revealed.

explainer.summary: Moses delivers the most hopeful chapter in Deuteronomy. Even after exile, if Israel returns to the Lord, God will restore them, gather them from the nations, and circumcise their hearts so they can truly love him. The command is not too difficult or far away -- it is near, in their mouths and hearts. Moses concludes with the ultimate choice: 'I have set before you life and death, blessings and curses. Now choose life.'
themes: Restoration after exile -- hope beyond judgment
God will circumcise their hearts
The command is near, not distant
Choose life -- the final appeal
Love God, for he is your life
explainer.contextInBook: Chapter 30 is the theological climax of Deuteronomy. After the devastating curses of chapter 28, this chapter offers hope: even if the worst happens, restoration is possible. And the choice itself is not impossibly difficult -- it is accessible, near, achievable.

wordStudies.significance: The word 'chayyim' (life) appears in the plural in Hebrew, suggesting fullness and abundance of life. When Moses says 'choose life,' he means choose flourishing, vitality, and relationship with God -- not mere biological existence.

verses.text: It shall happen, when all these things have come on you, the blessing and the curse, which I have set before you, and you shall call them to mind among all the nations, where Yahweh your God has driven you,
verses.explainer: When all these blessings and curses I have set before you come on you and you take them to heart wherever the Lord your God disperses you among the nations --

verses.text: and return to Yahweh your God, and obey his voice according to all that I command you today, you and your children, with all your heart, and with all your soul;
verses.explainer: And when you and your children return to the Lord your God and obey him with all your heart and with all your soul according to everything I command you today --

verses.text: that then Yahweh your God will release you from captivity, have compassion on you, and will return and gather you from all the peoples where Yahweh your God has scattered you.
verses.explainer: Then the Lord your God will restore your fortunes and have compassion on you and gather you again from all the nations where he scattered you.

crossReferences.reference: Jeremiah 29:14 -- 'I will be found by you, declares the Lord, and will bring you back from captivity. I will gather you from all the nations and places where I have banished you.'
crossReferences.connection: Jeremiah 29:14 -- 'I will be found by you, declares the Lord, and will bring you back from captivity. I will gather you from all the nations and places where I have banished you.'

verses.text: If your outcasts are in the uttermost parts of the heavens, from there Yahweh your God will gather you, and from there he will bring you back.
verses.explainer: Even if you have been banished to the most distant land under the heavens, from there the Lord your God will gather you and bring you back.

verses.text: Yahweh your God will bring you into the land which your fathers possessed, and you will possess it. He will do you good, and increase your numbers more than your fathers.
verses.explainer: He will bring you to the land that belonged to your ancestors, and you will take possession of it. He will make you more prosperous and numerous than your ancestors.

verses.text: Yahweh your God will circumcise your heart, and the heart of your offspring, to love Yahweh your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, that you may live.
verses.explainer: The Lord your God will circumcise your hearts and the hearts of your descendants, so that you may love him with all your heart and with all your soul, and live.

crossReferences.reference: Jeremiah 31:33 -- 'I will put my law in their minds and write it on their hearts.' God solves the problem of disobedience not by changing the law but by changing the heart.
crossReferences.connection: Jeremiah 31:33 -- 'I will put my law in their minds and write it on their hearts.' God solves the problem of disobedience not by changing the law but by changing the heart.

crossReferences.reference: Ezekiel 36:26 -- 'I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you; I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.'
crossReferences.connection: Ezekiel 36:26 -- 'I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you; I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh.'

verses.text: Yahweh your God will put all these curses on your enemies, and on those who hate you, who persecuted you.
verses.explainer: The Lord your God will put all these curses on your enemies who hate and persecute you.

verses.text: You shall return and obey Yahweh’s voice, and do all his commandments which I command you today.
verses.explainer: You will again obey the Lord and follow all his commands I am giving you today.

verses.text: Yahweh your God will make you plenteous in all the work of your hand, in the fruit of your body, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your ground, for good; for Yahweh will again rejoice over you for good, as he rejoiced over your fathers;
verses.explainer: Then the Lord your God will make you most prosperous in all the work of your hands and in the fruit of your womb, the young of your livestock and the crops of your land. The Lord will again delight in you and make you prosperous, just as he delighted in your ancestors.

verses.text: if you will obey Yahweh your God’s voice, to keep his commandments and his statutes which are written in this book of the law; if you turn to Yahweh your God with all your heart, and with all your soul.
verses.explainer: If you obey the Lord your God and keep his commands and decrees that are written in this Book of the Law and turn to the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

verses.text: For this commandment which I command you today is not too hard for you or too distant.
verses.explainer: Now what I am commanding you today is not too difficult for you or beyond your reach.

thenVsNow.then: Moses preemptively answers the excuse: 'This is too hard.' He insists the command is accessible. The covenant does not require superhuman effort -- it requires a willing heart.
thenVsNow.now: The claim that God's commands are not 'too difficult' must be balanced with the recognition (v.6) that God must circumcise the heart to enable obedience. The command is accessible, but the capacity to obey is a gift.

verses.text: It is not in heaven, that you should say, “Who will go up for us to heaven, and bring it to us, and proclaim it to us, that we may do it?”
verses.explainer: It is not up in heaven, so that you have to ask, 'Who will ascend into heaven to get it and proclaim it to us so we may obey it?'

crossReferences.reference: Romans 10:6-8 -- Paul applies these verses to the gospel: 'The word is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart.' He interprets the accessible command as the message of faith in Christ.
crossReferences.connection: Romans 10:6-8 -- Paul applies these verses to the gospel: 'The word is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart.' He interprets the accessible command as the message of faith in Christ.

verses.text: Neither is it beyond the sea, that you should say, “Who will go over the sea for us, and bring it to us, and proclaim it to us, that we may do it?”
verses.explainer: Nor is it beyond the sea, so that you have to ask, 'Who will cross the sea to get it and proclaim it to us so we may obey it?'

verses.text: But the word is very near to you, in your mouth and in your heart, that you may do it.
verses.explainer: No, the word is very near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart so you may obey it.

verses.text: Behold, I have set before you today life and prosperity, and death and evil.
verses.explainer: See, I set before you today life and prosperity, death and destruction.

verses.text: For I command you today to love Yahweh your God, to walk in his ways, and to keep his commandments, his statutes, and his ordinances, that you may live and multiply, and that Yahweh your God may bless you in the land where you go in to possess it.
verses.explainer: For I command you today to love the Lord your God, to walk in obedience to him, and to keep his commands, decrees, and laws; then you will live and increase, and the Lord your God will bless you in the land you are entering to possess.

verses.text: But if your heart turns away, and you will not hear, but are drawn away, and worship other gods, and serve them;
verses.explainer: But if your heart turns away and you are not obedient, and if you are drawn away to bow down to other gods and worship them --

verses.text: I denounce to you today, that you will surely perish. You will not prolong your days in the land where you pass over the Jordan to go in to possess it.
verses.explainer: I declare to you this day that you will certainly be destroyed. You will not live long in the land you are crossing the Jordan to enter and possess.

verses.text: I call heaven and earth to witness against you today, that I have set before you life and death, the blessing and the curse. Therefore choose life, that you may live, you and your descendants;
verses.explainer: This day I call the heavens and the earth as witnesses against you that I have set before you life and death, blessings and curses. Now choose life, so that you and your children may live.

verses.text: to love Yahweh your God, to obey his voice, and to cling to him; for he is your life, and the length of your days; that you may dwell in the land which Yahweh swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give them.
verses.explainer: And that you may love the Lord your God, listen to his voice, and hold fast to him. For the Lord is your life, and he will give you many years in the land he swore to give to your fathers, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.

thenVsNow.then: The final equation is stunning: 'The Lord is your life.' Not 'the Lord gives you life' (though that is true) but 'the Lord IS your life.' God himself, not the land or the law, is the ultimate good.
thenVsNow.now: The identification of God with life itself transforms every theological discussion. The choice is not between rules and freedom but between God (who is life) and everything else (which leads to death).

explainer.summary: Moses announces that he is 120 years old and can no longer lead. He publicly commissions Joshua, commanding him to be strong and courageous. Moses writes down the law and entrusts it to the Levites, commanding that it be read publicly every seven years at the Feast of Tabernacles. God tells Moses that Israel will indeed break the covenant after his death, and commands him to write a song (chapter 32) as a witness against them.
themes: Moses' farewell and Joshua's commissioning
The law written and publicly read
God's prediction of Israel's future apostasy
The Song of Moses as prophetic witness
Transition of leadership
explainer.contextInBook: Chapter 31 is the transition chapter: Moses' authority passes to Joshua, the written law passes to the Levites, and the prophetic song is prepared for Israel. Everything is being arranged for after Moses' death.

wordStudies.significance: The charge 'be strong and courageous' appears three times in this chapter (vv.6,7,23). It became Joshua's defining commission, repeated by God himself in Joshua 1:6-9. The repetition suggests that Joshua's primary temptation will be fear, and his primary need will be divine courage.

verses.text: Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel.
verses.explainer: Then Moses went out and spoke these words to all Israel.

verses.text: He said to them, “I am one hundred twenty years old today. I can no more go out and come in. Yahweh has said to me, ‘You shall not go over this Jordan.’
verses.explainer: He said: 'I am now a hundred and twenty years old and I am no longer able to lead you. The Lord has said to me, You shall not cross the Jordan.'

verses.text: Yahweh your God himself will go over before you. He will destroy these nations from before you, and you shall dispossess them. Joshua will go over before you, as Yahweh has spoken.
verses.explainer: The Lord your God himself will cross over ahead of you. He will destroy these nations before you, and you will take possession of their land. Joshua also will cross over ahead of you, as the Lord said.

verses.text: Yahweh will do to them as he did to Sihon and to Og, the kings of the Amorites, and to their land; whom he destroyed.
verses.explainer: And the Lord will do to them what he did to Sihon and Og, the kings of the Amorites, whom he destroyed along with their land.

verses.text: Yahweh will deliver them up before you, and you shall do to them according to all the commandment which I have commanded you.
verses.explainer: The Lord will deliver them to you, and you must do to them all that I have commanded you.

verses.text: Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid or scared of them; for Yahweh your God himself is who goes with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you.”
verses.explainer: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the Lord your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you.

crossReferences.reference: Hebrews 13:5-6 -- The author of Hebrews quotes this promise: 'God has said, Never will I leave you; never will I forsake you. So we say with confidence, The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid.'
crossReferences.connection: Hebrews 13:5-6 -- The author of Hebrews quotes this promise: 'God has said, Never will I leave you; never will I forsake you. So we say with confidence, The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid.'

verses.text: Moses called to Joshua, and said to him in the sight of all Israel, “Be strong and courageous, for you shall go with this people into the land which Yahweh has sworn to their fathers to give them; and you shall cause them to inherit it.
verses.explainer: Then Moses summoned Joshua and said to him in the presence of all Israel, 'Be strong and courageous, for you must go with this people into the land that the Lord swore to their ancestors to give them, and you must divide it among them as their inheritance.'

thenVsNow.then: The public commissioning ensures that all Israel witnesses the transfer of authority. There can be no question about who leads after Moses. The ceremony prevents power struggles.
thenVsNow.now: Clear, public leadership transitions prevent confusion and conflict. The principle of openly acknowledged succession applies to every organization.

verses.text: Yahweh himself is who goes before you. He will be with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be discouraged.”
verses.explainer: The Lord himself goes before you and will be with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.

verses.text: Moses wrote this law, and delivered it to the priests the sons of Levi, who bore the ark of Yahweh’s covenant, and to all the elders of Israel.
verses.explainer: So Moses wrote down this law and gave it to the Levitical priests, who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and to all the elders of Israel.

verses.text: Moses commanded them, saying, “At the end of every seven years, in the set time of the year of release, in the feast of tents,
verses.explainer: Then Moses commanded them: 'At the end of every seven years, in the year for canceling debts, during the Festival of Tabernacles --'

verses.text: when all Israel has come to appear before Yahweh your God in the place which he will choose, you shall read this law before all Israel in their hearing.
verses.explainer: When all Israel comes to appear before the Lord your God at the place he will choose, you shall read this law before them in their hearing.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: Public reading of the entire law every seven years ensured that each generation heard the full covenant. This was a national educational event coinciding with the most joyful festival, linking learning with celebration.

verses.text: Assemble the people, the men and the women and the little ones, and the foreigners who are within your gates, that they may hear, and that they may learn, and fear Yahweh your God, and observe to do all the words of this law;
verses.explainer: Assemble the people -- men, women and children, and the foreigners residing in your towns -- so they can listen and learn to fear the Lord your God and follow carefully all the words of this law.

verses.text: and that their children, who have not known, may hear, and learn to fear Yahweh your God, as long as you live in the land where you go over the Jordan to possess it.”
verses.explainer: Their children, who do not know this law, must hear it and learn to fear the Lord your God as long as you live in the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess.

verses.text:
Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, your days approach that you must die. Call Joshua, and present yourselves in the Tent of Meeting, that I may commission him.”
Moses and Joshua went, and presented themselves in the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: The Lord says to Moses: 'The day of your death is near. Call Joshua and present yourselves at the tent of meeting, where I will commission him.' So Moses and Joshua come and present themselves at the tent of meeting.

verses.text: Yahweh appeared in the Tent in a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood over the Tent’s door.
verses.explainer: Then the Lord appears at the tent in a pillar of cloud, and the cloud stands over the entrance to the tent.

verses.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Behold, you shall sleep with your fathers. This people will rise up, and play the prostitute after the strange gods of the land, where they go to be among them, and will forsake me, and break my covenant which I have made with them.
verses.explainer: And the Lord says to Moses: 'You are going to rest with your ancestors, and these people will soon prostitute themselves to the foreign gods of the land they are entering. They will forsake me and break the covenant I made with them.'

verses.text: Then my anger shall be kindled against them in that day, and I will forsake them, and I will hide my face from them, and they shall be devoured, and many evils and troubles shall come on them; so that they will say in that day, ‘Haven’t these evils come on us because our God is not among us?’
verses.explainer: And in that day I will become angry with them and forsake them; I will hide my face from them, and they will be destroyed. Many disasters and calamities will come on them, and in that day they will ask, 'Have not these disasters come on us because our God is not among us?'

verses.text: I will surely hide my face in that day for all the evil which they have done, in that they have turned to other gods.
verses.explainer: And I will certainly hide my face in that day because of all their wickedness in turning to other gods.

thenVsNow.then: God 'hiding his face' is the most terrifying consequence in the covenant. It means the withdrawal of God's protective presence -- not God's absence from the universe but his deliberate withdrawal from the relationship.
thenVsNow.now: The experience of God's hidden face (hester panim) has been a central theme in Jewish theology, especially after the Holocaust. The concept acknowledges that God may withdraw perceptibly while remaining sovereign.

verses.text: “Now therefore write this song for yourselves, and teach it to the children of Israel. Put it in their mouths, that this song may be a witness for me against the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: Now write down this song and teach it to the Israelites and have them sing it, so that it may be a witness for me against them.

verses.text: For when I have brought them into the land which I swore to their fathers, flowing with milk and honey, and they have eaten and filled themselves, and grown fat; then they will turn to other gods, and serve them, and despise me, and break my covenant.
verses.explainer: When I have brought them into the land flowing with milk and honey, the land I promised on oath to their ancestors, and when they eat their fill and thrive, they will turn to other gods and worship them, rejecting me and breaking my covenant.

verses.text: It will happen, when many evils and troubles have come on them, that this song will testify before them as a witness; for it will not be forgotten out of the mouths of their descendants; for I know their ways and what they are doing today, before I have brought them into the land which I swore.”
verses.explainer: And when many disasters and calamities come on them, this song will testify against them, because it will not be forgotten by their descendants. I know what they are disposed to do, even before I bring them into the land I promised them on oath.

verses.text: So Moses wrote this song the same day, and taught it to the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: So Moses writes down this song that day and teaches it to the Israelites.

verses.text: He commissioned Joshua the son of Nun, and said, “Be strong and courageous; for you shall bring the children of Israel into the land which I swore to them. I will be with you.”
verses.explainer: The Lord gives this command to Joshua son of Nun: 'Be strong and courageous, for you will bring the Israelites into the land I promised them on oath, and I myself will be with you.'

crossReferences.reference: Joshua 1:5-9 -- God repeats this commissioning after Moses' death, expanding it with the charge to meditate on the law 'day and night.'
crossReferences.connection: Joshua 1:5-9 -- God repeats this commissioning after Moses' death, expanding it with the charge to meditate on the law 'day and night.'

verses.text: When Moses had finished writing the words of this law in a book, until they were finished,
verses.explainer: After Moses finishes writing in a book the words of this law from beginning to end --

verses.text: Moses commanded the Levites, who bore the ark of Yahweh’s covenant, saying,
verses.explainer: He gives this command to the Levites who carry the ark of the covenant of the Lord:

verses.text: “Take this book of the law, and put it by the side of the ark of Yahweh your God’s covenant, that it may be there for a witness against you.
verses.explainer: Take this Book of the Law and place it beside the ark of the covenant of the Lord your God. There it will remain as a witness against you.

verses.text: For I know your rebellion, and your stiff neck. Behold, while I am yet alive with you today, you have been rebellious against Yahweh. How much more after my death?
verses.explainer: For I know how rebellious and stiff-necked you are. If you have been rebellious against the Lord while I am still alive and with you, how much more will you rebel after I die!

verses.text: Assemble to me all the elders of your tribes, and your officers, that I may speak these words in their ears, and call heaven and earth to witness against them.
verses.explainer: Assemble before me all the elders of your tribes and all your officials, so that I can speak these words in their hearing and call the heavens and the earth to testify against them.

verses.text: For I know that after my death you will utterly corrupt yourselves, and turn away from the way which I have commanded you; and evil will happen to you in the latter days; because you will do that which is evil in Yahweh’s sight, to provoke him to anger through the work of your hands.”
verses.explainer: For I know that after my death you are sure to become utterly corrupt and to turn from the way I have commanded you. In days to come, disaster will fall on you because you will do evil in the sight of the Lord and arouse his anger by what your hands have made.

verses.text: Moses spoke in the ears of all the assembly of Israel the words of this song, until they were finished.
verses.explainer: And Moses recites the words of this song from beginning to end in the hearing of the whole assembly of Israel.

explainer.summary: The Song of Moses is the longest poem in the Pentateuch -- a prophetic masterpiece that tells the whole story of God and Israel from creation to final vindication. It begins with praise of God's perfection, recounts his care for Israel, describes Israel's ingratitude and idolatry, announces the resulting judgment, and concludes with God's ultimate vindication of his people and vengeance on their enemies. The chapter ends with God commanding Moses to ascend Mount Nebo to view the land and die.
themes: God is the Rock -- faithful and perfect
Israel's ingratitude for God's care
Jeshurun grew fat and kicked -- prosperity breeds rebellion
God's jealousy aroused by their idolatry
Final vindication and atonement for the land
explainer.contextInBook: The Song functions as a prophetic witness (31:19-21). When Israel sins, they cannot claim ignorance -- the song they memorized predicted exactly what would happen. It serves as both indictment and hope, ending with atonement for the land.

wordStudies.significance: God is called 'the Rock' six times in this song. The image conveys stability, reliability, and permanence. Israel's unfaithfulness is contrasted with God's rock-like faithfulness. The 'rock' of the nations' gods is explicitly declared inferior (v.31).
'Yeshurun' is a poetic name for Israel, derived from 'yashar' (upright/straight). The irony is intentional: the 'upright one' grows fat and rebels. The ideal name contrasts with the actual behavior.

verses.text:
Give ear, you heavens, and I will speak.
Let the earth hear the words of my mouth.
verses.explainer: Listen, you heavens, and I will speak; hear, you earth, the words of my mouth.

verses.text:
My doctrine will drop as the rain.
My speech will condense as the dew,
as the misty rain on the tender grass,
as the showers on the herb.
verses.explainer: Let my teaching fall like rain and my words descend like dew, like showers on new grass, like abundant rain on tender plants.

verses.text:
For I will proclaim Yahweh’s name.
Ascribe greatness to our God!
verses.explainer: I will proclaim the name of the Lord. Oh, praise the greatness of our God!

verses.text:
The Rock, his work is perfect,
for all his ways are just.
A God of faithfulness who does no wrong,
just and right is he.
verses.explainer: He is the Rock, his works are perfect, and all his ways are just. A faithful God who does no wrong, upright and just is he.

crossReferences.reference: Romans 9:14 -- Paul asks 'Is God unjust?' and answers emphatically 'Not at all!' -- reasoning from the same theological foundation as this verse.
crossReferences.connection: Romans 9:14 -- Paul asks 'Is God unjust?' and answers emphatically 'Not at all!' -- reasoning from the same theological foundation as this verse.

verses.text:
They have dealt corruptly with him.
They are not his children, because of their defect.
They are a perverse and crooked generation.
verses.explainer: They are corrupt and not his children; to their shame they are a warped and crooked generation.

thenVsNow.then: The contrast between God's perfection (v.4) and Israel's corruption (v.5) is immediate and devastating. The 'warped and crooked generation' language echoes throughout the prophets.
thenVsNow.now: Philippians 2:15 borrows this language: Paul urges believers to 'become blameless and pure, children of God without fault in a warped and crooked generation.'

verses.text:
Is this the way you repay Yahweh,
foolish and unwise people?
Isn’t he your father who has bought you?
He has made you and established you.
verses.explainer: Is this the way you repay the Lord, you foolish and unwise people? Is he not your Father, your Creator, who made you and formed you?

verses.text:
Remember the days of old.
Consider the years of many generations.
Ask your father, and he will show you;
your elders, and they will tell you.
verses.explainer: Remember the days of old; consider the generations long past. Ask your father and he will tell you, your elders, and they will explain to you.

verses.text:
When the Most High gave to the nations their inheritance,
when he separated the children of men,
he set the bounds of the peoples
according to the number of the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance, when he divided all mankind, he set up boundaries for the peoples according to the number of the sons of Israel.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: Some ancient manuscripts read 'sons of God' instead of 'sons of Israel' -- the Dead Sea Scrolls and the Septuagint support this reading. This suggests that God assigned heavenly beings over each nation but kept Israel as his own direct portion.

verses.text:
For Yahweh’s portion is his people.
Jacob is the lot of his inheritance.
verses.explainer: For the Lord's portion is his people, Jacob his allotted inheritance.

crossReferences.reference: Psalm 33:12 -- 'Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people he chose for his inheritance.'
crossReferences.connection: Psalm 33:12 -- 'Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord, the people he chose for his inheritance.'

verses.text:
He found him in a desert land,
in the waste howling wilderness.
He surrounded him.
He cared for him.
He kept him as the apple of his eye.
verses.explainer: In a desert land he found him, in a barren and howling waste. He shielded him and cared for him; he guarded him as the apple of his eye.

verses.text:
As an eagle that stirs up her nest,
that flutters over her young,
he spread abroad his wings,
he took them,
he bore them on his feathers.
verses.explainer: Like an eagle that stirs up its nest and hovers over its young, that spreads its wings to catch them and carries them aloft.

verses.text:
Yahweh alone led him.
There was no foreign god with him.
verses.explainer: The Lord alone led him; no foreign god was with him.

verses.text:
He made him ride on the high places of the earth.
He ate the increase of the field.
He caused him to suck honey out of the rock,
oil out of the flinty rock;
verses.explainer: He made him ride on the heights of the land and fed him with the fruit of the fields. He nourished him with honey from the rock, and with oil from the flinty crag.

verses.text:
butter from the herd, and milk from the flock,
with fat of lambs,
rams of the breed of Bashan, and goats,
with the finest of the wheat.
From the blood of the grape, you drank wine.
verses.explainer: With curds and milk from herd and flock and with fattened lambs and goats, with choice rams of Bashan and the finest kernels of wheat. You drank the foaming blood of the grape.

verses.text:
But Jeshurun grew fat, and kicked.
You have grown fat.
You have grown thick.
You have become sleek.
Then he abandoned God who made him,
and rejected the Rock of his salvation.
verses.explainer: Jeshurun grew fat and kicked; filled with food, they became heavy and sleek. They abandoned the God who made them and rejected the Rock their Savior.

verses.text:
They moved him to jealousy with strange gods.
They provoked him to anger with abominations.
verses.explainer: They made him jealous with their foreign gods and angered him with their detestable idols.

verses.text:
They sacrificed to demons, not God,
to gods that they didn’t know,
to new gods that came up recently,
which your fathers didn’t dread.
verses.explainer: They sacrificed to false gods, which are not God -- gods they had not known, gods that recently appeared, gods your ancestors did not fear.

crossReferences.reference: 1 Corinthians 10:20 -- Paul cites this concept: 'The sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to be participants with demons.'
crossReferences.connection: 1 Corinthians 10:20 -- Paul cites this concept: 'The sacrifices of pagans are offered to demons, not to God, and I do not want you to be participants with demons.'

verses.text:
Of the Rock who became your father, you are unmindful,
and have forgotten God who gave you birth.
verses.explainer: You deserted the Rock, who fathered you; you forgot the God who gave you birth.

verses.text:
Yahweh saw and abhorred,
because of the provocation of his sons and his daughters.
verses.explainer: The Lord saw this and rejected them because he was angered by his sons and daughters.
He said: 'I will hide my face from them and see what their end will be; for they are a perverse generation, children who are unfaithful.'

verses.text:
They have moved me to jealousy with that which is not God.
They have provoked me to anger with their vanities.
I will move them to jealousy with those who are not a people.
I will provoke them to anger with a foolish nation.
verses.explainer: They made me jealous by what is no god and angered me with their worthless idols. I will make them envious by those who are not a people; I will make them angry by a nation that has no understanding.

crossReferences.reference: Romans 10:19 -- Paul quotes this verse to explain why Gentiles are receiving the gospel: God is provoking Israel to jealousy through a people who were 'not a people.'
crossReferences.connection: Romans 10:19 -- Paul quotes this verse to explain why Gentiles are receiving the gospel: God is provoking Israel to jealousy through a people who were 'not a people.'

verses.text:
For a fire is kindled in my anger,
that burns to the lowest Sheol,
devours the earth with its increase,
and sets the foundations of the mountains on fire.
verses.explainer: For a fire will be kindled by my wrath, one that burns down to the realm of the dead below. It will devour the earth and its harvests and set afire the foundations of the mountains.

verses.text:
“I will heap evils on them.
I will spend my arrows on them.
verses.explainer: I will heap calamities on them and spend my arrows against them.

verses.text:
They shall be wasted with hunger,
and devoured with burning heat
and bitter destruction.
I will send the teeth of animals on them,
with the venom of vipers that glide in the dust.
verses.explainer: I will send wasting famine against them, consuming pestilence and deadly plague; I will send against them the fangs of wild beasts, the venom of vipers that glide in the dust.

verses.text:
Outside the sword will bereave,
and in the rooms, terror;
on both young man and virgin,
the nursing infant with the gray-haired man.
verses.explainer: In the street the sword will make them childless; in their homes terror will reign. The young men and young women will perish, the infants and the gray-haired.

verses.text:
I said that I would scatter them afar.
I would make their memory to cease from among men;
verses.explainer: I said I would scatter them and erase their name from human memory.

verses.text:
were it not that I feared the provocation of the enemy,
lest their adversaries should judge wrongly,
lest they should say, ‘Our hand is exalted,
Yahweh has not done all this.’”
verses.explainer: But I dreaded the taunt of the enemy, lest the adversary misunderstand and say: Our hand has triumphed; the Lord has not done all this.

verses.text:
For they are a nation void of counsel.
There is no understanding in them.
verses.explainer: They are a nation without sense, there is no discernment in them.

verses.text:
Oh that they were wise, that they understood this,
that they would consider their latter end!
verses.explainer: If only they were wise and would understand this and discern what their end will be!

verses.text:
How could one chase a thousand,
and two put ten thousand to flight,
unless their Rock had sold them,
and Yahweh had delivered them up?
verses.explainer: How could one man chase a thousand, or two put ten thousand to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, unless the Lord had given them up?

verses.text:
For their rock is not as our Rock,
even our enemies themselves concede.
verses.explainer: For their rock is not like our Rock, as even our enemies concede.

thenVsNow.then: Even Israel's enemies recognize the superiority of Israel's God. The comparison between the 'rock' of the nations and the Rock of Israel is not close -- even outsiders acknowledge the difference.
thenVsNow.now: The admission from outside the faith community that Israel's God is superior echoes throughout biblical narrative (Rahab, Naaman, Nebuchadnezzar) and challenges the assumption that only insiders can recognize truth.

verses.text:
For their vine is of the vine of Sodom,
of the fields of Gomorrah.
Their grapes are poison grapes.
Their clusters are bitter.
verses.explainer: Their vine comes from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are filled with poison, and their clusters with bitterness.

verses.text:
Their wine is the poison of serpents,
the cruel venom of asps.
verses.explainer: Their wine is the venom of serpents, the deadly poison of cobras.

verses.text:
“Isn’t this laid up in store with me,
sealed up among my treasures?
verses.explainer: 'Have I not kept this in reserve and sealed it in my vaults?'

verses.text:
Vengeance is mine, and recompense,
at the time when their foot slides;
for the day of their calamity is at hand.
Their doom rushes at them.”
verses.explainer: It is mine to avenge; I will repay. In due time their foot will slip; their day of disaster is near and their doom rushes upon them.

crossReferences.reference: Romans 12:19 -- Paul quotes this: 'Do not take revenge, my dear friends, but leave room for God's wrath, for it is written: It is mine to avenge; I will repay, says the Lord.'
crossReferences.connection: Romans 12:19 -- Paul quotes this: 'Do not take revenge, my dear friends, but leave room for God's wrath, for it is written: It is mine to avenge; I will repay, says the Lord.'

verses.text:
For Yahweh will judge his people,
and have compassion on his servants,
when he sees that their power is gone;
that there is no one remaining, shut up or left at large.
verses.explainer: The Lord will vindicate his people and relent concerning his servants when he sees their strength is gone and no one is left, slave or free.

verses.text:
He will say, “Where are their gods,
the rock in which they took refuge;
verses.explainer: He will say: 'Now where are their gods, the rock they took refuge in?'

verses.text:
which ate the fat of their sacrifices,
and drank the wine of their drink offering?
Let them rise up and help you!
Let them be your protection.
verses.explainer: The gods who ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up to help you! Let them give you shelter!

verses.text:
“See now that I myself am he.
There is no god with me.
I kill and I make alive.
I wound and I heal.
There is no one who can deliver out of my hand.
verses.explainer: See now that I myself am he! There is no god besides me. I put to death and I bring to life, I have wounded and I will heal, and no one can deliver out of my hand.

verses.text:
For I lift up my hand to heaven and declare,
as I live forever,
verses.explainer: I lift my hand to heaven and solemnly swear: As surely as I live forever --
When I sharpen my flashing sword and my hand grasps it in judgment, I will take vengeance on my adversaries and repay those who hate me.

verses.text:
I will make my arrows drunk with blood.
My sword shall devour flesh with the blood of the slain and the captives,
from the head of the leaders of the enemy.”
verses.explainer: I will make my arrows drunk with blood, while my sword devours flesh: the blood of the slain and the captives, the heads of the enemy leaders.

verses.text:
Rejoice, you nations, with his people,
for he will avenge the blood of his servants.
He will take vengeance on his adversaries,
and will make atonement for his land and for his people.
verses.explainer: Rejoice, you nations, with his people, for he will avenge the blood of his servants; he will take vengeance on his enemies and make atonement for his land and people.

crossReferences.reference: Romans 15:10 -- Paul quotes this verse in his argument that the Gentiles are included in God's purposes: 'Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people.'
crossReferences.connection: Romans 15:10 -- Paul quotes this verse in his argument that the Gentiles are included in God's purposes: 'Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people.'

verses.text: Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people, he and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.explainer: Moses comes with Joshua son of Nun and speaks all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.

verses.text: Moses finished reciting all these words to all Israel.
verses.explainer: When Moses finishes reciting all these words to all Israel --

verses.text: He said to them, “Set your heart to all the words which I testify to you today, which you shall command your children to observe to do, all the words of this law.
verses.explainer: He says to them: 'Take to heart all the words I have solemnly declared to you this day, so that you may command your children to obey carefully all the words of this law.'
